annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

tools.description: Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.

body.description: Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

state_reason.description: The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.

tools.name: close_issue

tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization

message.description: The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols

owners.description: Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')

tools.name: mention_owners

content.text: {"number":58255,"title":"[Conversations Authoring] Docs: Document new sub-client architecture in README and CHANGELOG","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring` **Service directory:** `sdk/cognitivelanguage/Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring/` **Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (PR #58177) by `@jorgerangel-msft` ## What Changed PR #58177 introduced four new resource-oriented sub-client classes as part of a major API refactoring of the Conversations Authoring SDK: - **`ConversationAuthoringProject`** — project-level operations (create, delete, import, export, train, swap deployments, assign/unassign resources, etc.) - **`ConversationAuthoringDeployment`** — deployment-level operations (deploy, get, delete, delete from resources, etc.) - **`ConversationAuthoringTrainedModel`** — trained model operations - **`ConversationAuthoringExportedModel`** — exported model operations The main `ConversationAnalysisAuthoring` client was significantly refactored (3,591 deletions, 16 additions), and collection result classes were renamed. All sample test files were updated to use the new sub-client pattern. ## Gaps Found - **CHANGELOG `1.0.0-beta.4 (Unreleased)` is completely empty** — the `Features Added`, `Breaking Changes`, `Bugs Fixed`, and `Other Changes` sections are all blank despite the introduction of four new sub-clients and a major refactoring of the primary client - **README `Key concepts` section does not mention the new sub-clients** — the section only references `ConversationAnalysisAuthoring` as the primary client; the new resource-oriented sub-client hierarchy (`ConversationAuthoringProject`, `ConversationAuthoringDeployment`, `ConversationAuthoringTrainedModel`, `ConversationAuthoringExportedModel`) is not described - **README SDK version table is out of date** — the table only includes entries through `1.0.0-beta.3`; `1.0.0-beta.4` is missing along with its supported API versions - **No samples exist for the new sub-clients** — all existing samples still use the flat `ConversationAnalysisAuthoring` pattern; there are no samples demonstrating how to obtain and use the new sub-clients 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above ### Step 1: Modify files #### File 1: `sdk/cognitivelanguage/Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring/CHANGELOG.md` Edit the existing `1.0.0-beta.4 (Unreleased)` section to document the new sub-clients and breaking changes: ```markdown ## 1.0.0-beta.4 (Unreleased) ### Features Added - Added new resource-oriented sub-clients for more granular API access: - `ConversationAuthoringProject` — provides project-level operations including create, get, delete, import, export, train, cancel training, swap deployments, assign/unassign project resources, and resource status polling. - `ConversationAuthoringDeployment` — provides deployment-level operations including deploy project, get deployment, delete deployment, delete deployment from resources, and resource deletion status polling. - `ConversationAuthoringTrainedModel` — provides trained model operations including get, delete, load snapshot, and model evaluation. - `ConversationAuthoringExportedModel` — provides exported model operations. ### Breaking Changes - The primary `ConversationAnalysisAuthoring` client has been significantly refactored. Many operations previously available directly on `ConversationAnalysisAuthoring` are now accessed via the new sub-clients (`ConversationAuthoringProject`, `ConversationAuthoringDeployment`, `ConversationAuthoringTrainedModel`, `ConversationAuthoringExportedModel`). - Collection result classes for trained model evaluation results have been renamed for consistency. ``` #### File 2: `sdk/cognitivelanguage/Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring/README.md` **A) Update the SDK version table** (in the "Install the package" section) to add the `1.0.0-beta.4` row. Locate this table: ```markdown |SDK version |Supported API version of service |-------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------- |1.0.0-beta.1 | 2023-04-01, 2023-04-15-preview, 2024-11-15-preview (default) |1.0.0-beta.2 | 2023-04-01, 2023-04-15-preview, 2024-11-15-preview, 2025-05-15-preview (default) |1.0.0-beta.3 | 2023-04-01, 2025-11-01, 2025-05-15-preview, 2025-11-15-preview (default) ``` Replace with: ```markdown |SDK version |Supported API version of service |-------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------- |1.0.0-beta.1 | 2023-04-01, 2023-04-15-preview, 2024-11-15-preview (default) |1.0.0-beta.2 | 2023-04-01, 2023-04-15-preview, 2024-11-15-preview, 2025-05-15-preview (default) |1.0.0-beta.3 | 2023-04-01, 2025-11-01, 2025-05-15-preview, 2025-11-15-preview (default) |1.0.0-beta.4 | 2023-04-01, 2025-11-01, 2025-05-15-preview, 2025-11-15-preview (default) ``` > **Note:** Verify the exact supported API versions for `1.0.0-beta.4` by checking `sdk/cognitivelanguage/Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring/src/Generated/ConversationAnalysisAuthoringClientOptions.cs` (or equivalent options class) before finalizing. **B) Update the `Key concepts` → `ConversationAnalysisAuthoring Client` section** to describe the sub-client architecture. Replace the existing paragraph with: ```markdown ### ConversationAnalysisAuthoring Client The [ConversationAnalysisAuthoring][ConversationAnalysisAuthoring_class] is the top-level entry point for the Azure AI Conversation Authoring client library. It provides both synchronous and asynchronous operations and serves as the factory for the following resource-oriented sub-clients: - **`ConversationAuthoringProject`** — create, get, delete, import, export, and train conversation projects; assign and unassign project resources; swap deployments. - **`ConversationAuthoringDeployment`** — deploy projects, get and delete deployments, and manage deployment resource assignments. - **`ConversationAuthoringTrainedModel`** — get and delete trained models; load snapshots; retrieve evaluation summaries and results. - **`ConversationAuthoringExportedModel`** — manage exported model snapshots. Obtain sub-client instances from the primary client (check the generated client for the exact factory method names, e.g., `GetConversationAuthoringProjectClient()`). ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets Add a new sample test file demonstrating the new sub-client pattern: **Test file path:** `sdk/cognitivelanguage/Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring/tests/Samples/Sample24_ConversationsAuthoring_SubClientArchitecture.cs` ```csharp // Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved. // Licensed under the MIT License. using System; using System.Threading.Tasks; using Azure; using Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring; using Azure.Core.TestFramework; using NUnit.Framework; namespace Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring.Tests.Samples { public partial class ConversationsAuthoringClientLiveTest : RecordedTestBase { [Test] [SyncOnly] public void Sample24_SubClientArchitecture() { #region Snippet:ConversationsAuthoring_GetSubClients Uri endpoint = new Uri("{endpoint}"); AzureKeyCredential credential = new AzureKeyCredential("{api-key}"); ConversationAnalysisAuthoring client = new ConversationAnalysisAuthoring(endpoint, credential); // Obtain resource-oriented sub-clients from the primary client ConversationAuthoringProject projectClient = client.GetConversationAuthoringProjectClient(); ConversationAuthoringDeployment deploymentClient = client.GetConversationAuthoringDeploymentClient(); ConversationAuthoringTrainedModel trainedModelClient = client.GetConversationAuthoringTrainedModelClient(); #endregion } } } ``` > **Note:** Check the generated `ConversationAnalysisAuthoring.cs` for the exact factory method names for obtaining sub-clients before finalizing the snippet. **README location:** Insert a new entry in the `Examples` section listing the new sample, and add a snippet placeholder right after the `Key concepts` sub-client architecture description: ``` ``` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README has all required sections. The following items need updating: 1. **Getting started → Install the package**: Update the SDK version table to include `1.0.0-beta.4` (see Step 1A above). 2. **Key concepts → ConversationAnalysisAuthoring Client**: Expand to describe the sub-client hierarchy (see Step 1B above). 3. **Examples**: Add a link to the new `Sample24` demonstrating how to obtain sub-clients. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/cognitivelanguage/Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/cognitivelanguage/Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 cognitivelanguage` 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 cognitivelanguage` 5. `dotnet format sdk/cognitivelanguage/Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24545958946/agentic_workflow) · ● 637.7K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58255","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-17T03:28:18Z","updated_at":"2026-04-17T03:28:18Z"}

arguments.query: repo:Azure/azure-sdk-for-net label:\"Cognitive - Language\" label:\"Client\" label:customer-reported label:issue-addressed

arguments.message: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

arguments.body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This bot-filed issue identifies documentation gaps in the `Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring` SDK introduced by PR #58177, specifically missing CHANGELOG entries, outdated README content, and absent samples for the new resource-oriented sub-client architecture.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring` (1.0.0-beta.4 unreleased)
**Affected API:** `ConversationAnalysisAuthoring`, `ConversationAuthoringProject`, `ConversationAuthoringDeployment`, `ConversationAuthoringTrainedModel`, `ConversationAuthoringExportedModel`
**Scenarios:**
- CHANGELOG `1.0.0-beta.4 (Unreleased)` section is empty despite major refactoring and four new sub-clients
- README `Key concepts` section does not reference the new sub-client hierarchy
- README SDK version table is missing the `1.0.0-beta.4` row
- No samples exist demonstrating the new sub-client pattern

**Root ask:** Update CHANGELOG, README (Key concepts + version table), and add a sample demonstrating how to obtain and use the new resource-oriented sub-clients.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a documentation gap issue filed by automation after commit `[REDACTED]` (PR #58177) introduced a major API refactoring. The issue provides an implementation guide with specific file edits.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Review PR #58177 to confirm all new sub-client classes and renamed types introduced in the refactoring.
2. Update `sdk/cognitivelanguage/Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring/CHANGELOG.md` with Features Added and Breaking Changes per the implementation guide.
3. Update `sdk/cognitivelanguage/Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring/README.md` — SDK version table and Key concepts sub-client section.
4. Verify exact factory method names in generated `ConversationAnalysisAuthoring.cs` before writing samples.
5. Run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 cognitivelanguage` and `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 cognitivelanguage` to validate.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Client` — The issue concerns a data plane client SDK (`Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring`), not a management or provisioning library.
- **Service:** `Cognitive - Language` — The package resides in `/sdk/cognitivelanguage/` and maps directly to the `Cognitive - Language` ServiceLabel in CODEOWNERS.
- **Confidence:** High — The package is explicitly named in the issue, the service directory is unambiguous, and the category is clearly Client (data plane). No competing labels considered.
</details>

<details>
<summary>👥 Owner Routing</summary>

- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %Cognitive - Language` (line 190) — the predicted service label `Cognitive - Language` matches this entry exactly.
- **AzureSdkOwners:** none listed
- **ServiceOwners:** @quentinRobinson
- **Routing action:** Added `Service Attention` label; posted @mention to ServiceOwners. Issue not assigned (no AzureSdkOwners). `needs-team-attention` not added (issue is bot-filed, not customer-reported).
- **Scan notes:** Bottom-to-top scan reached the `%Cognitive - Language` entry at line 190 as the first match. No more-specific multi-label entries exist for this service. Entries for unrelated services (e.g., `%Cognitive - Personalizer`, `%Cognitive - Text Analytics`) were skipped as they don't include `Cognitive - Language`.
</details>
